steps.title: Keep subject next to verb
steps.content: The blank supplies the subject of the verb "found." A title directly before a person's name forms one noun phrase, and standard English does not place punctuation between a subject and its verb.

steps.title: Remove interrupting punctuation
steps.content: A puts a colon between the subject and verb. B incorrectly sets off the name with commas and still separates subject from verb. D inserts a comma between subject and verb. The answer is C.
